course_title: Live Theatre Production: Performance Creation & Event Management
course_description:
This course is a practical exploration of stylistic, theoretical, and historical questions through rehearsal and performance of a live theatre event. Students will work practically across the semester in a work simulation to rehearse and perform a full production, under the leadership of an experienced director. This course will develop the skills required to present an independent theatre production, as well as acting and performance skills that can be applied elsewhere in the UQ Drama program and beyond.

This course aims to familiarise students with the production process of a live performance event, through participating in an on-campus performance.ﾠThe primary mode of study in this course is experiential; that is, students will be working practically on the rehearsal room floor in block rehearsal pattern at the beginning of the semester. The course can therefore be understood as a 'work simulation': students are developing their experience in working in professional ways, with particular reference to independence theatre practice. The 2026 production is Australian Drama by David Magaritty and directed by Dr Bridget Boyle.

timetable:
The timetable for this course is available on the
UQ Public Timetable
.
Additional timetable information
Regular classes will be held from Monday 9th February until Saturday 7th March. The first two weeks are scheduled from Monday to Friday 10.00am to 2.00pm, the third week is scheduled Monday to Friday 3:00pm to 7:00pm, and Saturday 10:00am to 8:00pm. The final week is scheduled from Monday to Saturday 4:00pm to 9:00pm as the production moves into performance block.
Attendance at all rehearsals, tech/dress runs, performances and debrief is compulsory. Students will need to organise work and other study to fit around this demanding schedule. This is a practicum course designed to instil professional ethics and practices in its participants. Absence will be heavily penalised. If you think you cannot commit to the timetable, it is best that you withdraw from the course now and make other study plans.
All rehearsals for the course will be held in the Avalon Theatre. From time to time, an additional rehearsal may be called, with due consideration to existing commitments.
Students will also need to make arrangements with their work and other extracurricular commitments to meet the rehearsal schedule.
course_aims: This course aims to familiarise students with the production process of a live performance event, through participating in an on-campus performance. Students will make an independent contribution to the performance event, both on- and off-stage, and collaborate with the cohort as a whole to realise all elements of performance and production. Ordinarily, this performance will be presented in the Geoffrey Rush Drama Studio, however from time to time a venue more appropriate to the form of the performance event will be selected. Students may also collaborate with industry professionals as directors and mentors as appropriate. As well as completing research into the critical and theoretical context of the performance event, students will learn the practical skills normally associated with live performance, including acting, design elements, publicity, marketing, event management, publicity, fundraising, stage management, and directing.ﾠ

learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate an ability to work with a group to achieve a joint performance project
Develop and apply specific industry skills relating to live performance and production
Make an independent contribution to a live performance event
Analyse and apply performance methodologies and theories in a production context

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 3 (09 Feb - 01 Mar)
learning_activities.topic: Development and Rehearsal Process Students will rehearse the realisation of a live performance event, along with all necessary supporting production elements. Attendance at all of these sessions is compulsory. All students in the course are required from 10am-2pm for the first 2 rehearsal weeks (from 09/02 to 20/02) and 3pm-7pm in the third rehearsal week (from 23/02 to 27/02). On Saturday, 28/02, students are required from 10am-8pm. There will be no rehearsals on public holidays.

Production Week & Performances The entire work is prepared for presentation, including rigging of lights and installation of set, and then performances (including Saturday). All students in the course are required from 4-9pm on Monday through Saturday of performance week. Students may be required Sunday, 01 March.

policies_and_procedures: University policies and procedures apply to all aspects of student life. As a UQ student, you must comply with University-wide and program-specific requirements, including the: Student Code of Conduct Policy Student Integrity and Misconduct Policy and Procedure Assessment Procedure Examinations Procedure Reasonable Adjustments for Students Policy and Procedure AI for Assessment Guide Learn more about UQ policies on my.UQ and the Policy and Procedure Library . Course guidelines Communication Expectations While you are a student at UQ, all communication must be conducted according to the UQ Student Code of Conduct . The UQ Library has a helpful Communicate and collaborate online module . Email is the primary way for you to send messages to, and receive information from, the School and our staff. You must use your UQ email address (not a private address) to communicate with staff. You should add a clear subject line, including course code, and a 2-3 word statement. You can send email at any time, however please do not expect responses outside normal working hours (Monday to Friday from ~8am to ~5pm). Emails that constitute bullying, harassment or discrimination against staff contravene the Student Code of Conduct. Emails like this will be reported to the University, and the matter will be pursued as misconduct. Learning activities
